attacking_style: Pure sprinter who relies on positioning and raw speed in final 200m rather than launching early. Lacks tactical maturity in deciding when to initiate sprint, often waiting too long or going too early.
team_tactics: Dependent on team leadout but frequently left exposed or isolated in final kilometers. Multiple instances of losing team support before finale (Catalunya 4km solo, various crashes) suggesting weak train coordination or positioning.
terrain_preference: Flat, technical finales with tailwind approaches where positioning matters more than power. Eliminated on any sustained climbing (30km+ ascending routes, steep late-race climbs). Surprisingly competitive on short uphill finishes (2km gradients).
vulnerability: Crash-prone in technical roundabout-heavy finales with poor positioning outside top positions. Cannot survive extended climbs or tempo on ascents. Tactically indecisive on when to launch sprint, costing podiums and wins.
key_patterns: Eliminated by climbing tempo on stages with 30km+ ascending or steep late climbs (3 instances)
Crash or mechanical issues inside final 10km destroy sprint opportunities repeatedly (3 documented cases)
Excels when field fractures in crosswinds/splits, eliminating pure sprinters (2 wins from reduced bunches)
Poor tactical decision-making: premature pulls or delayed sprints cost certain victories (2+ clear examples)
Performs above expectation on short uphill finishes where power sprinters struggle (2km gradients)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 showed first World Tour win breakthrough but ended with career-threatening shoulder fracture. Pattern of crashes and injuries persists.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 4
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 4
